AT 945 AM EST SUWANNEE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGMENT REPORTED POWER OUT ALL OVER THE CITY OF LIVE OAK. POST EVENT RADAR ANALYSIS AND FOLLOW UP WITH EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT INDICATES EVENT STARTED NEAR 920 AM EST AND LASTED UNTIL 935 AM EST.

*** 2 FATAL *** ONE DIRECT FATALITY WAS DUE TO TREE DOWN ON MOBILE HOME. FEMALE FATALITY WAS IN MOBILE HOME AT 649 NE GIBBS TERRACE. ONE INDIRECT FATALITY WAS DUE TO A POWER SURGE BUT DETAILS OF LOCATION OF EVENT ARE UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME.
PUBLIC REPORTED A POSSIBLE TORNADO ON THEIR PROPERTY AT 5401 NW 22ND CT IN BELL. FOUR TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
ONE RESIDENCE WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED AND ONE RESIDENCE HAD TIN ROOF BLOWN OFF DUE TO POSSIBLE TORNADO. LOCATIONS WERE ALONG O.C HORNE ROAD AND GOLDEN GRASS FARMS LANE RESPECTIVELY.

A TOTAL OF 5 HOUSES WERE DAMAGED WITH ONE HOUSE SUSTAINING MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. DAMAGE PATH ESTIMATED BY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT TO BE 1 MILE IN LENGTH. POSSIBLE TORNADO INDUCED DAMAGE WAS ALONG STATE ROAD 185 NEAR MILE MARKER 2.

A SPOTTER REPORTED A SWATH OF TREES SNAPPED ACROSS A FARM ALONG STATE ROAD 185 NEAR EXIT 5. THERE WERE NUMEROUS PINE TREES SNAPPED OFF ABOUT 8-10 FEET ABOVE GROUND LEVEL AND THE TOPS WERE STREWN ABOUT FARMLAND. LARGE CYPRESS TREES WERE DAMAGED AND TWISTED. SNAPPED TREE TOPS WERE OBSERVED IN POWER LINES WHICH CAUSED AREA POWER OUTAGES. THE TIME OF THE DAMAGE WAS BASED ON DOPPLER RADAR.
POWER POLES TWISTED AND SNAPPED. ROOF BLOWN OFF HOUSE.
A COUPLE OF TREES BLOWN DOWN IN TROY SPRINGS STATE PARK. TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON REPORT AND RADAR IMAGERY.

EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT RPORTED A ONE HALF MILE LONG AND 200 YARD WIDE DAMAGE PATH THROUGH A WOODED AREA NEAR POST ROAD AND BOONES BRANCH ROAD. SIGNIFICANT TREE BLOWDOWN OCCURED AND THE ROAD DEPARTMENT WAS DISPACHED TO CLEAR THE ROADWAY. SEVERAL TRAP TYPE GARAGES WERE DAMAGED AND THE TORNADO TRANSITED BETWEEN SEVERAL HOMES. REPORT RECEIVED 3-11-08 1410 EDT.

START OF TORNADO TRACK AT NW STADIUM TERRACE IN WEST HILLSBORO WHERE IT WAS RATED AN EF1. TREES AND BLEACHERS BLOWN DOWN WITH STRUCTUAL HOME DAMAGE. A POWER POLE SNAPPED IN HALF. AN ABANDONED METAL WAREHOUSE RIPPED APART.

*** 2 FATAL *** ONE DIRECT FATALITY WAS DUE TO TREE DOWN ON MOBILE HOME. FEMALE FATALITY WAS IN MOBILE HOME AT 649 NE GIBBS TERRACE. ONE INDIRECT FATALITY WAS DUE TO A POWER SURGE BUT DETAILS OF LOCATION OF EVENT ARE UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME.

A NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM HAS DETERMINED EF2 TORNADO DAMAGE IN LAKE CITY. PRELIMINARY REPORTS INDICATE THAT THE VORTEX WAS A QUARTER MILE WIDE AND TRAVELED AROUND A HALF MILE ON THE GROUND. HOMES WERE UN-ROOFED AND EXTERIOR WALLS COLLAPSED ALONG NORTHEAST DENVER ROAD. UPDATED STORM REPORTS WILL BE ISSUED AS INFORMATION BECOMES AVAILABLE. THE TIME OF DAMAGE WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR. INJURIES AND FATALITIES ARE UNKNOWN.

A NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM IS ASSESSING SIGNIFICANT TORNADO DAMAGE IN LAKE CITY. AN ENHANCED FUJITA SCALE MAGNITUDE HAS NOT BEEN DETERMINED YET. INITIAL SURVEY REPORTS INCLUDE ABOUT 20 HOMES WITH SIGNIFICANT STRUCTURAL DAMAGE AND NUMEROUS MOBILE HOMES DESTROYED NEAR 1700 BASCON NORRIS DRIVE IN LAKE CITY. MORE STORM REPORTS WILL BE ISSUED WITH FURTHER DETAILS AS INFORMATION BECOMES AVAILABLE. THE TIME OF DAMAGE WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.INJURIES AND FATALITIES ARE UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME.

WINDS ESTIMATED AT 60 MPH DESTROYED A PUMP HOUSE WHICH SUPPLIES WATER FOR THE MAIN HOUSE.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A LARGE TREE ON A HOUSE ON ORANGE STREET IN HILLIARD.
A RETIRED NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A BILLBOARD DESTROYED NEAR MARINERS VILLAGE AROUND 6 MILES EAST OF INTERSTATE 95 NEAR HIGHWAY 40. TIME GIVEN WAS BASED ON RADAR.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ON HIGHWAY 40 2 MILES EAST OF FOLKSTON.
RETIRED NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED THAT SEVERAL TREES HAD THE TOPS SNAPPED OFF OF THEM NEAR THE WEIGH STATION NEAR INTERSTATE 95. TIME GIVEN WAS BASED ON RADAR.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED AN OAK TREE UPROOTED AT THE HARBOR PINES APARTMENTS ON HARBOR PINES DRIVE.
FORMER NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTS NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS DOWN AND ESTIMATED WINDS OF 60 MPH IN BRISTOL HAMMOCK SUBDIVISION.

TREE WAS UPROOTED AND FELL ACROSS HOUSE ON BLOSSOM RIDGE DRIVE NORTH. TIME IS APPROXIMATE BASED ON RADAR.
CHARLTON COUNTY EMA REPORTED 22 LARGE TREES BLOWN DOWN DURING THE EVENT. THE DOWNED TREES LANDED ON FOUR HOMES...TWO BUSINESSES AND FOUR TRAILORS. THE EVENT LASTED FROM 315 TO 327 PM EDT.
A TRAINED SPOTTER ON COUNTY ROAD 235 REPORTED 2 SMALL PINE TREES DOWN ON HIS PROPERTY, TREE DIAMETER ABOUT 10 INCHES.

JACKSONVILLE PORT AUTHORITY OFFICIALS REPORT TWO LARGE 125 FOOT TALL CONTAINER SHIP CRANES COLLAPSED AT BLOUNT ISLAND MARINE TERMINAL AT APPROXIMATELY 330 PM EDT. A SQUALL LINE WAS MOVING ACORSS THE AREA WHEN THE COLLASPE OCCURED. WINDS AT CRAIG AIRPORT...SOUTHEAST OF THE MARINE TERMINAL GUSTED TO 45 KNOTS AS THE SQUALL LINE TRANSITED THE AREA. PRELIMINARY ANALYSIS OF KJAX DOPPLER RADAR BASE VELOCITY DATA INDICATED 45 TO 50 KNOT WINDS AT APPROXIMATELY 600 FEET MSL. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED.
EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTS NUMEROUS POWERLINES DOWN OVER THE EASTERN HLAF OF DUVAL COUNTY.
NUMEROUS POWERLINES WERE REPORTED DOWN IN THE NEWBERRY AREA.
THE EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED A TREE DOWN ACROSS ROADWAY ON PONY LANE. REPORTED BY FCSO DISPATCH.
NWS SURVEY INDICATED MCCOYS CREEK AT KING STREET FLASH FLOODED. IN ADDITION 2 VEHICLES FLOODED WITH 1 OF THEM WITH WATER UP TO THE ROOF. 2 BUILDINGS WERE ALSO FLOODED.
LOCAL BROADCAST MEDIA SHOWED IMAGES OF SEVERE STREET FLOODING ALONG THE MONCREIF CREEK BASIN. WATER WAS WAIST HIGH AND STREETS WERE IMPASSABLE DUE TO FLOODED ROADWAYS.

FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED ALONG MONCRIEF CREEK WITH ONE OF THE MOST SIGNIFICANT RISES IN WATER EXPERIENCED ALONG THE CREEK. AT 209 PM EDT THE SUMMER BROOK HEALTH CARE CENTER AT 5377 MONCRIEF ROAD WAS EVACUATED WITH WATER SURROUNDING THE FACILITY. DURING THE FLOODING EVENT OF OCTOBER 2ND 2008 WATER ENTERED THE GROUNDS BUT DID NOT THREATEN THE FACILITY.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED A TRONADO ON THE GROUND NORTH NORTHEAST OF HASTINGS.
THE TOPS OF PINE TREES WERE SNAPPED OFF AT SAN PABLO SUBDIVISION.

EMERGENCY RESPONSE OFFICIALS ARE RESPONDING TO TORNADO DAMAGE AT THE KANGAROO EXPRESS ALONG CHURCH STREET IN HASTINGS. THE DAMAGE OCCURRED AROUND 435 PM BASED ON WEAK DOPPER RADAR ROTATION SIGNATURE. A TORNADO PATH IS EVIDENT IN THE AREA AND EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT SAID THAT THEY HAVE A PICTURE OF THE FUNNEL CLOUD PRIOR OR JUST AFTER TOUCHDOWN IN THE HASTINGS AREA.
A TORNADIC WATERSPOUT WAS ON THE SAINT JOHNS RIVER NORTHEAST OF BOSTWICK TRACKING NNW. PICTURES HAVE BEEN SENT TO THE NWS.
POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE AT 1287 FLOYD STREET ON FLEMING ISLAND. WIDESPRED TREE DAMAGE AND DAMAGE TO RESIDENCE WELL. REPORT RELAYED TO NWS BY STATE WARNING POINT.
PUBLIC REPORT OF A WATERSPOUT OVER THE ST JOHNS RIVER NEAR GREEN COVE SPRINGS.

A STORM SPOTTER OBSERVED A TORNADO PATH BEGINNING ON THE SOUTH END OF FLEMING ISLAND. THE CIRCULATION TRACKED NNW OVER THE ISLAND MAKING BREIEF TOUCHDOWNS ACROSS HIGHWAY 17 AND THE SOUTH END OF BLACK CREEK TRAIL...CROSSING OVER THE SOUTHERN PART OF THE FLEMING ISLAND PLANTATION COMPLEX AND THEN OLD HARD ROAD. MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG THIS PATH. THE SPOTTER REPORTED SEEING A BROAD AREA OF DEBRIS APPROACHING HIS HOME AND HEARING A JET ENGINE SOUND NEAR THE RIVER HILLS SUBDIVISION AT 555 PM.
WIDESPREAD REPORTS OF TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN WERE RECIEVED BY ALACHUA COUNTY SHERIFF DISPATCH.
TWO TREES WERE DOWN ACROSS COUNTY ROAD 135.
TWO TREES WERE DOWN ACROSS COUNTY ROAD 135.

A SEMINOLE ELECTRIC EMPLOYEE REPORTED A TORNADO NEAR 870 NORTH HIGHWAY 17 MOVING NORTH TOWARD BOSTWICK. UNCONFIRMED EVENT...POSSIBLY A WATERSPOUT MOVING INLAND OFF THE RIVER. REPORT WAS RELAYED TO NWS BY FLORIDA STATE WARNING POINT.
PUBLIC REPORTS A TORNADO ON THE GROUND NEAR WEST RIVER ROAD AND MINK ROAD. THE PARENT THUNDERSTORM WAS MOVING NORTH ALONG U.S. HIGHWAY 17 TOWARD THE BOSTWICK AREA.

REPORTS OF TREE DAMAGE CONTINUES IN GAINESVILLE. MOST RECENT REPORT IS OF A TREE DOWN AT THE INTERSECTION OF A SUBDIVISION AT 5113 NW 33RD STREET WHICH HAS BLOCKED ACCESS TO THAT DEVELOPMENT.

NUMEROUS REPORTS OF TREES DOWN ACROSS GAINESVILLE. THE EVENT IS ONGOING WITH MORE REPORTS COMING IN. REPORTS HAVE BEEN RECEIVED FROM THE NW...NE AND SE PORTIONS OF THE CITY.

CAMDEN EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED A LARGE TREE DOWN IN TARBORO, POWER OUT TO LOCAL COMMUNITY. ALSO TREES DOWN IN DOVER BLUFF.
A NWS STORM SURVEY REVEALED THAT LAKE HAMPTON ROAD IS CLOSED DUE TO FLOOD WATERS LEAVING THE LAKE. MOST OF THE WATER THAT IS LEAVING HAMPTON LAKE IS FLOWING INTO PIGEON CREEK AT A STEADY RATE.

A STORM SPOTTER REPORTED SEEING A TORNADO WHICH GENERATED DEBRIS THAT RESULTED IN DAMAGE TO HIS CAR WINDSHIELD WHILE DRIVING NEAR NEW POST ROAD AND HIGHWAY 252. THE SPOTTER ALSO REPORTED TWO TREES DOWN IN ASSOCIATION WITH THE TORNADO.
